name: Graves and Garonne Loop
name_original: Port d'Isle-Saint-Georges - boucle par les vignobles des Graves
description: Cycle through the Graves vineyards from Port d'Isle-Saint-Georges on this moderate 46-mile loop, exploring wine country and charming village
region_intro: Touring cycling around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans offers routes through a landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, humid bocage areas, and riverside forests. The region features gentle terrain, with elevations generally under 200 meters, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Cyclists can explore areas influenced by the Garonne river and its associated wetlands, alongside the Gât-Mort valley. This blend of viticultural heritage and natural environments provides diverse scenery for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans The most popular touring cycling route is Graves and Garonne Loop, a 46.0 miles (74.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route traverses the renowned Graves vineyards and follows sections near the Garonne river. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gravel Road by the Quarry loop from Saint-Medard d'Eyrans, a moderate 25.9 miles (41.7 km) path. This route offers varied terrain, including gravel sections, through the local landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Blue Lake – Lac Bleu loop from Saint-Medard d'Eyrans, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through natural areas around a lake, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans is defined by vineyards, bocage, and riverside forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans?
A: Touring cycling in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans offers a diverse range of scenery. You'll primarily cycle through extensive vineyards, particularly those of the prestigious Pessac-Léognan appellation, alongside humid bocage areas and riverside forests influenced by the Garonne river. The region also features wetlands with small streams and ditches, providing a rich natural environment.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans?
A: Yes, Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans offers routes suitable for various abilities, including easy options perfect for beginners and families. The terrain is generally gentle, with elevations typically under 200 meters. An example of an easier route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15221117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Isle-Saint-Georges loop from Saint-Médard-d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated easy and covers about 17 km.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I discover along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history and culture. You can encounter several notable landmarks, such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cayac Priory&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval monument that served pilgrims on the Way of St. James. Nearby, you might also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1484682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;"La Reine de la Rive" statue&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also dotted with wine-producing châteaux and Mission Crosses erected between 1858 and 1888, reflecting its deep viticultural heritage.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10710323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake – Lac Bleu loop from Saint-Medard d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular journey through natural areas around a lake.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans?
A: The region's mild climate makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially during the grape harvest or when the vineyards are in full bloom. Morning rides are often recommended for picturesque views.

Q: Can I find routes that include natural water features or lakes?
A: Absolutely. The region is influenced by the Garonne river and features several natural water bodies. You can explore routes that lead to places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Springs Pond&lt;/a&gt;, which is known for its biodiversity and is often traversed by cycle paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7062340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens lake beach – Shaded section loop from Saint-Médard-d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent option for routes featuring lakes and shaded areas.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The touring cycling routes around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans are predominantly moderate, with some easy and a few difficult options. The overall terrain is gentle, making it accessible for most cyclists. Out of 23 available routes, 15 are rated moderate, 4 are easy, and 4 are difficult, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a mix of terrain, such as gravel sections?
A: Yes, some routes provide varied terrain for a more adventurous touring cycling experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1403527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Road by the Quarry loop from Saint-Medard d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt; offers sections of gravel, allowing you to explore different surfaces within the local landscape.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans?
A: The touring cycling experience in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque vineyard landscapes, the gentle terrain, and the diverse natural environments that make for enjoyable and accessible rides.

Q: Are there any specific cycling paths or trails known for leisure cycling?
A: Yes, the area is well-suited for 'vélo plaisir' (leisure cycling). The 'Piste Roger Lapébie' is described as ideal for enjoyable cycling, offering easily accessible trails. Additionally, routes like the 'Boucle dans les vignobles' provide an immersive and leisurely experience through the vineyards.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Saint-Médard-D'Eyrans, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the region's vineyards, bocage, and riverside forests.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Port d'Isle-Saint-Georges loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 74 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 hours and 25 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling time and does not include stops for sightseeing or wine tasting.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route through the Graves vineyards?
faqs.answer: The terrain is best described as rolling countryside. While much of it is on quiet country roads, you can expect some 'lumpy' sections with occasional ascents and descents. The route also includes a Gravel Path Along the Airfield, adding variety to the surface.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this route is suitable for cyclists with some experience. While it's not overly strenuous, the distance of 74 kilometers and the rolling terrain mean it's more challenging than a completely flat, short ride. Beginners with good fitness could manage it, but might find it a long day.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Port d'Isle-Saint-Georges loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich experience through the Graves wine region. You'll pass through the Port of Isle-Saint-Georges, cycle along sections of the Hostens Cycle Path – Endless Forests, and encounter numerous vineyards, charming villages, and historic châteaux. The unique gravel soil of Graves is also a notable feature of the landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the start point in Port d'Isle-Saint-Georges are not provided, it's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the village or near the Port itself. As a popular cycling region, local amenities often cater to visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle through the Graves vineyards?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically from spring to early autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is generally pleasant for cycling, and the vineyards are lush. Autumn offers the added beauty of harvest season and vibrant foliage.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on public roads and paths in the region, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific rules may apply if you enter private vineyard properties or certain natural reserves. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route through the Graves vineyards. You are free to explore the public roads and designated cycle paths.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the EV3 France 40, Bordeaux - Hostens, and the Piste Cyclable Hostens - La Brède. You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Rocade de Bordeaux and various local loops like the Boucle locale d'Arbanats and Boucle locale de Cérons.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat, drink, or find accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as you cycle through the Graves wine region, you'll pass through various villages and near châteaux. These areas often have opportunities for wine tastings, local cuisine, and cafes. For accommodation, consider staying in one of the charming villages or in nearby Bordeaux.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling attire. Sunscreen and a hat are recommended, especially during warmer months. Don't forget your camera to capture the stunning vineyard scenery and historic sites.
